data.title: Analysts: Bitcoin price leads the M2 indicator; the DXY indicator is more suitable for assessing its correlation with Bitcoin.
data.description: Crypto analyst Willy Woo wrote, “The market doesn’t follow the expansion of global M2 supply; instead, it’s speculative. This means the market prices in anticipated liquidity in advance. Risk assets tend to lead M2. Therefore, at global peaks, you’ll see the S&P 500 peak earlier, and Bitcoin will peak even earlier. This is because Bitcoin acts as a liquidity-sensing mechanism. Furthermore, M2 is a flawed indicator because when you assess the total amount of fiat currency in the system, you’re measuring it in US dollars, but only about 17% is actually in US dollars; the rest is foreign currency. So M2 actually reflects more the strength of the US dollar. A more suitable indicator has proven to be the DXY (US Dollar Index), which is far more reliable when assessing its correlation with Bitcoin.”

data.title: Gates spot trading volume hit a new high of $163 billion in October, a 39.1% increase month-over-month.
data.description: Gate.com maintained its position as the world's second-largest spot exchange in October, with spot trading volume reaching $163 billion, a 39.1% increase month-over-month, setting a new record. Its global spot market share also rose to 6.04%, a 0.57 percentage point increase from the previous month, also a record high, second only to Binance (28.6%). Since the beginning of 2025, Gate.com's spot market share has increased by 3.43 percentage points year-to-date, making it the fastest-growing centralized exchange. In the derivatives market, global derivatives trading volume surged 25.8% to $7.56 trillion in October, a new high for the year; Gate.com maintained its fourth-largest global market share with a 10.5% market share. Meanwhile, Gate.com's global open interest accounted for 9.92%, further solidifying its leading position in the industry. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Disney (DIS) Beats Q4 EPS but Falls Short on Revenue
data.description:
Disney reported Q4 EPS of $1. 11 alongside $22. 5 billion in revenue, topping profit expectations but falling short on sales, with growth expected in 2026.

data.title: Bitdeer Plans $400 Million Convertible Senior Notes Offering
data.description:
According to PANews, Bitdeer (Nasdaq: BTDR) has announced its intention to privately issue $400 million in Convertible Senior Notes due in 2031. The company is offering initial purchasers an option to increase the issuance by up to $60 million. The interest rate and initial conversion rate will be determined based on pricing, with conversion consideration available in cash, Class A common stock, or a combination of both.

The funds raised are intended for several purposes, including entering into capped call transactions with option counterparties to hedge dilution, repurchasing its 5.25% convertible bonds due in 2029, expanding data centers, developing and manufacturing ASIC mining machines, expanding HPC and AI cloud businesses, and providing operational capital.

Bitdeer is also advancing a registered direct offering to partially repurchase the 2029 convertible bonds, with the completion of related transactions being interdependent.

data.title: Ant Group Considers Extending Acquisition Deadline
data.description: According to Odaily, Hong Kong's Bright Smart Securities has announced that Ant Group, the acquisition offeror, is contemplating the possibility of extending the final deadline for their transaction. The deal requires approval from the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission and the National Development and Reform Commission. However, it is currently uncertain if all transaction conditions can be met by the November 25 deadline.

data.title: Bitdeer Plans $400 Million Fundraising Through Convertible Notes
data.description: According to Odaily, Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer has announced its intention to raise $400 million through the issuance of convertible senior notes. The company also plans to grant initial purchasers of the notes an option to buy up to an additional $60 million within 13 days of the initial issuance. This could increase the total fundraising amount to $460 million. The new funds are intended for the expansion of data centers, development of ASIC mining machines, expansion of AI and HPC cloud business, and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Emory University Expands Bitcoin ETF Holdings Amid Market Shifts
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Emory University, a prominent private research institution in Georgia, has significantly increased its investment in Grayscale Investments' Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF. The university's holdings have surpassed one million shares, valued at approximately $51.8 million, as revealed in the quarterly Form 13F report submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission. This marks a substantial increase from the second quarter of the year, with Emory doubling its position by acquiring an additional 487,636 shares worth around $25 million.

Emory University initially entered the Bitcoin ETF market in October 2024, becoming one of the first U.S. universities to disclose such holdings. At that time, it reported a $15 million stake in the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF. The Mini Trust ETF, launched in July 2024 under the ticker symbol BTC, is a derivative of Grayscale's original Bitcoin Trust ETF (GBTC), which began trading in January 2024. The Mini Trust ETF is distinguished by its lower annual management fee of 0.15%, compared to the 1.5% fee of the original GBTC ETF. It was introduced through an initial seeding process that involved distributing 10% of GBTC's underlying Bitcoin.

In addition to its substantial investment in Grayscale's Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF, Emory University holds 4,450 shares of BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin ETF (IBIT), valued at approximately $290,000. This investment was disclosed in the second quarter, with no changes reported since then. Grayscale's Bitcoin ETFs have experienced significant investor outflows, losing over $21.3 billion in 2024, according to CoinShares data. Conversely, BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin ETFs have been a major contributor to Bitcoin ETF inflows, accounting for at least 80% of the total inflows last year, estimated at $48.7 billion.

In 2025, Grayscale's Bitcoin ETFs have continued to face challenges, with $2.5 billion in outflows, while BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin ETFs have attracted $37.4 billion in inflows, as per the latest CoinShares update. This trend highlights the shifting dynamics within the Bitcoin ETF market, with Emory University's strategic investments reflecting broader market movements and investor preferences.

data.title: Emory University increased its holdings in Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF to $51.8 million.
data.description: Emory University increased its holdings in the Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust to over 1 million shares, worth approximately $51.8 million, according to a quarterly 13F filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday. Since the second quarter, the university has doubled its holdings in a Bitcoin ETF, adding 487,636 shares, worth approximately $25 million. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ant Group may postpone its acquisition of Bright Smart Securities, a Hong Kong-based stablecoin concept stock.
data.description: Hong Kong-based Bright Smart Securities announced that Ant Group, the offeror for the acquisition, is considering extending the final deadline for the transaction. The deal reportedly requires approval from the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission and the National Development and Reform Commission, but it may not be possible to meet all transaction conditions before November 25th. (Caixin)

data.title: Trump's disapproval rating climbed to 58%, while his approval rating remained at 40%.
data.description: Polls show Trump's approval rating remaining at 40%, while his disapproval rating has climbed to 58%. Trump previously stated that the government shutdown caused $1.5 trillion in losses, and that the full impact of the losses would take weeks or even months to calculate. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin News: Taiwan Premier Commits to Bitcoin Reserve Assessment Report by End of 2025
data.description: Key TakeawaysTaiwan will release an official report assessing the country’s seized Bitcoin holdings before the end of 2025.The report will evaluate the feasibility, benefits, and risks of establishing a national Bitcoin reserve.The move follows rising global government interest in Bitcoin, including the U.S. creation of a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve earlier this year.Taiwan is preparing an official assessment of its Bitcoin holdings, marking the government’s first formal step toward evaluating whether the country should establish a national Bitcoin reserve.Premier Zhuo Rongtai confirmed during a legislative fiscal inquiry that the government will publish a comprehensive report on all Bitcoin confiscated by domestic law enforcement agencies. The report is scheduled for completion before the end of 2025.The assessment will include an analysis of the “pros and cons” of creating a strategic Bitcoin reserve, as well as recommendations on whether Taiwan should hold, liquidate, or strategically reintegrate seized BTC into national reserves. This represents the first time Taiwanese officials have publicly considered Bitcoin as a potential reserve asset.Lawmakers Push to Hold Seized Bitcoin Instead of SellingDuring the inquiry, legislator Ge Rujun urged the government to preserve confiscated Bitcoin rather than sell it, proposing that Taiwan consider retaining the assets as part of a long-term reserve strategy.Legislator Ko Ju-Chun described the premier’s commitment as a “breakthrough,” noting in an X post that Taiwan’s next six months will focus on exploring friendlier Bitcoin regulations and studying the merit of Bitcoin-backed reserve strategies.Taiwan’s growing interest follows the United States’ formation of a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, signed into effect by President Donald Trump on March 7. The U.S. reserve is initially composed of Bitcoin forfeited in federal criminal cases.Growing Political Momentum for a Bitcoin ReserveCalls for a national Bitcoin reserve within Taiwan are not new. In May 2025, Ko Ju-Chun urged the government to allocate up to 5% of Taiwan’s $50 billion national reserve into Bitcoin, arguing that BTC can act as a hedge during periods of global economic uncertainty and geopolitical tension.Taiwanese policymakers say that analyzing global developments — especially U.S. policy changes — is essential to safeguarding financial stability and competitiveness.Taiwan Accelerates Crypto Regulatory DevelopmentTaiwan has been working to modernize its digital asset framework. In October 2024, the Financial Supervisory Commission (FSC) launched a pilot program allowing financial institutions to offer regulated crypto custody services. The move signaled Taiwan’s intent to strengthen institutional adoption and oversight.With the forthcoming Bitcoin reserve assessment, Taiwan is now positioning itself among a growing number of governments evaluating crypto reserves as part of broader monetary and national security planning.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: Government shutdown could affect December meeting
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell stated that a government shutdown could arguably impact the December meeting. In the absence of data, greater caution may be necessary. The lack of economic data could provide a reason to pause interest rate adjustments. According to CME FedWatch, the probability of a Fed rate cut in December is 55.9%, down from 66.9% a day ago. (Jinshi)

data.title: Australian Authorities Combat Sophisticated Cryptocurrency Scams
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, cryptocurrency scammers have been impersonating Australian police and exploiting government infrastructure to deceive victims into surrendering their digital assets, as reported by the Australian Federal Police (AFP) on Thursday. The scammers utilize the local cybercrime reporting tool, ReportCyber, to submit reports about their targets. Subsequently, they contact the victims, posing as police officers, and invite them to verify the report on government websites, thereby lending credibility to their fraudulent activities.

In one instance, the scammers informed the victim that they would be contacted by a representative from a cryptocurrency company, who would provide report information to establish legitimacy. This second caller then attempted to persuade the target to transfer funds from their platform wallet to a wallet of their choice. Fortunately, the target became suspicious and terminated the call, as noted by the AFP.

AFP Detective Superintendent Marie Andersson highlighted that the scammers falsely claimed an individual had been arrested and the victim identified in an investigation related to a cryptocurrency breach. She emphasized that the scammers' verification steps often mimic legitimate law enforcement procedures, making the scheme highly convincing to some victims. Andersson pointed out that this type of scam is part of a broader trend of increasingly sophisticated scams. She urged Australians to adopt necessary online safety measures and warned that if contacted about a ReportCyber report they did not lodge or authorize, they should terminate the call and notify ReportCyber. She also reminded the public that legitimate law enforcement officials will never request access to cryptocurrency accounts, wallets, bank accounts, cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases, or any personal information related to financial accounts.

Australian authorities have been actively addressing crypto crime. In late October, the AFP announced the successful cracking of a coded cryptocurrency wallet backup containing 9 million Australian dollars ($5.9 million), suspected to be proceeds of a crime. Additionally, in late August, Australia's markets regulator expanded its campaign against online scams, having dismantled 14,000 scams since July 2023, with over 3,000 involving cryptocurrency. In July, authorities in Tasmania discovered that the top 15 users of crypto ATMs in the state were all victims of scams, with combined losses amounting to 2.5 million Australian dollars ($1.6 million).

data.title: Balancer initiated a white-hat recovery effort and transferred approximately $4.1 million to a controlled escrow account.
data.description: Balancer issued a statement on the X platform stating that, regarding the recent Balancer V2 stablecoin pool incident, the Balancer team has initiated a white-hat recovery operation and transferred approximately $4.1 million in funds to a controlled escrow account for reconciliation and returns. This incident only affected the Balancer V2 stablecoin pool; Balancer V3 was unaffected. Balancer reminds community users to be wary of phishing attacks and to refrain from any operations with the listed pools for the time being. Further updates will be provided after reconciliation is completed.

data.title: India’s market watchdog eyes sweeping changes to attract foreign money
data.description:
India’s top financial watchdog said Wednesday it wants to make several big changes to bring overseas investors back to the stock market. Cut red tape. Lower trading fees. Make it simpler to borrow shares. Tuhin Kanta Pandey took over as chairman of the Securities and Exchange Board of India in March. Since then, he’s been […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/india-eyes-changes-to-attract-foreign-money/
